extraction.one_line_summary: GIRMAY Biniam became the first Eritrean/Black African to win a Giro stage, outsprinting VON DE POEL after late attacks from YATES and CARAPAZ failed in final 6km.
tactical_patterns: Pure sprinters YUEN and CAVENDISH dropped 76km out on minimal hills, revealing fragility when temperature spikes on coastal rolling terrain.
YATES attacked with 6km remaining on descent after final climb, signaling GC riders tested legs despite 'sprint stage' label.
INTERMARCHÉ controlled entirely for GIRMAY despite no intermediate sprint positioning attempts, showing team confidence in pure speed over lead-out perfection.

rider_signals.YUEN Caleb: negative: First pure sprinter distanced at 76km on trivial gradients in heat, suggesting poor recovery post-rest day or accumulating fatigue.
rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: neutral: Mechanical inside 60km then chase back suggests legs present but form/positioning unclear for stage outcome.
rider_signals.YATES Simon: positive: Despite losing 11 minutes previously, launched meaningful 6km attack with GC riders, indicating focus shifted to stage hunting not GC survival.
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: neutral: Active in final kilometers without committing full attack, testing sprint contenders more than seeking solo opportunity.
rider_signals.GIRMAY Biniam: positive: Beat VON DE POEL head-to-head again with minimal lead-out, confirming raw speed superiority over positioning-dependent sprinters.
